SPX 0DTE IF/THEN ROAD MAP
📍 Spot Ref: ~5690
🔸 Chop Zone = 5650–5710 (85-90 ish stall)
• IF > 5670 → path to 5690 (OVN high) – chop at 75–85
• IF < 5670–75 → retest 5660
• IF < 5660 → 5650–55 = reversal vs. continuation
🔸 Above Chop Zone
• IF > 5685–90 → battle begins at 5700
• IF > 5700 → 5710 stall → fade vs. grind
• IF > 5710 → grind to 5725 sticky zone
• IF > 5725 → slow push to 5740
• IF > 5740 → test 5750 stall zone
🔸 Below Chop Zone
• IF < 5650–55 → fast drop to 5635/30/25 (Wed close = 5631)
• IF = 5625–30 → bounce back to 5650 vs. drift to 5600 sticky
• IF < 5625 → vol spike risk → accelerated flows

** Review each Weekly Post every Monday (https://stochvoltrader.substack.com/s/market-analysis?utm_source=substack&utm_medium=menu)

---- New Daily Chat set up each day 
---- Trading Signals Chat (Alma provides trading signals occasionally as a courtesy - don't expect signals all the time! This chat was set up on February 3 soon after Alma opened up the Substack; link is https://substack.com/chat/2679129/post/5ec8d5aa-9fac-4e6d-aef1-3f6a28632ae4)

As you know, Alma made it clear she doesn’t see a directional edge on either side, which makes sense. comment.body: This is exactly what I wrote up above you, that you don’t understand the trade signal. Alma isn’t shorting the ES mini because doomsday is tomorrow. She gave us a clear, high-probability signal and said she sees 5720 as the top, which makes perfect sense based on the gamma chart. The market then pulled back 28 points, and since the puts were underpriced, they printed nicely. That’s a straightforward reversion trade. And in my read, using 1DTE options means two things. One, to dodge theta decay, since she wasn’t expecting a sharp reversal in a rangebound market that suppresses vol..exactly what she’s betting on. And two, if the move gains confirmation, this could mark the start of a slightly larger pullback within the range, especially since the UK didn’t buy the deal narrative. So please, don’t be so touchy, there’s no need to take everything as a personal attack

comment.body:
Originally, the dealer-flow model said that if volatility starts to fall above 5,709 the price action can climb the gamma. That’s because traders trade based on their positioning and want the price to go up. If volatility had broken out instead, that stabilizing effect wouldn’t have kicked in.
But when the put signal came in, she said that at 5,720 you need to pay attention to how sharply volatility is dropping, cause there was a big local long gamma exposure there.. and since that Trump pump was pretty fragile and looked ripe for mean reversion, even a small shock could break it. So if volatility eases here, it means the squeeze is over and the move is at the mercy of dealer flow..which, given the upside long gamma, means they’ll be selling
The contextual difference is really important. Hope that makes sense

Originally, the dealer-flow model said that if volatility starts to fall above 5,709 the price action can climb the gamma. That’s because traders trade based on their positioning and want the price to go up. If volatility had broken out instead, that stabilizing effect wouldn’t have kicked in.
But when the put signal came in, she said that at 5,720 you need to pay attention to how sharply volatility is dropping, cause there was a big local long gamma exposure there.. and since that Trump pump was pretty fragile and looked ripe for mean reversion, even a small shock could break it. So if volatility eases here, it means the squeeze is over and the move is at the mercy of dealer flow..which, given the upside long gamma, means they’ll be selling
The contextual difference is really important. Hope that makes sense
